campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 19, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council held a meeting on May 19, 2026, where they discussed staffing vacancies and the city's efforts to recruit and retain employees. The council also addressed public comments on various matters and noted that the Boogie Festival is happening this weekend.
keyTopics: Staffing vacancies and recruitment/retention efforts
Public hearing on government code section 3502
Boogie Festival this weekend
Public comment period on council items

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — June 3, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council met to decide which sheriff service level option to adopt for the next fiscal year. Once the council picks an approach, staff will provide further direction on implementing that choice.
keyTopics: Choosing between different sheriff service level options for FY 2026
Decision will guide next steps for sheriff services in Saratoga
Council to establish near-term sheriff department approach

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — May 19, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met to approve financial reports, authorize the town manager to sign contracts for road paving and other services, and apply for state grants to fund upcoming projects. The council also adopted a list of projects for the next fiscal year that will be funded through state money.
keyTopics: Approved road paving contract with O'Grady Paving
Authorized grant applications for state funding
Reviewed March 2026 financial and investment reports
Adopted Fiscal Year 2026-27 project list
Approved five-year service agreements with vendors

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 16, 2026
san-jose.summary: San José City Council met on June 16, 2026, to handle routine business including approving committee records, settling a claim with Gordon Biersch Brewing Company, and appointing people to city boards and commissions. The council also reviewed the Hotel Business Improvement District's annual report and assessed fees for next year, plus moved forward on acquiring property at 26-36 South First Street.
keyTopics: Settlement agreement with Gordon Biersch Brewing Company
Hotel Business Improvement District assessments for 2026-2027
Real property acquisition at 26-36 South First Street
Board and commission appointments
Approval of council committee minutes

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — June 9, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met on June 9, 2026, to handle a closed private session about hiring a new City Attorney, then reconvened publicly to officially recognize Pride Month and Juneteenth.
keyTopics: Hiring decision for City Attorney position (discussed in closed session)
Pride Month proclamation
Juneteenth proclamation

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — June 16, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The City Council held two back-to-back special meetings on June 16, 2026: a closed session (details not public) followed by a study session focused on reviewing the Community Equity Assessment.
keyTopics: Community Equity Assessment update and discussion
Closed session meeting (specific agenda not publicly disclosed)

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — June 15, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a closed-door meeting with legal counsel to discuss an anticipated legal case. The council also reviewed what was discussed in that private session and reminded the public about rules requiring lobbyists to register with the city.
keyTopics: One anticipated litigation case discussed in closed session with legal counsel
Public reporting on closed session discussion
Lobbyist registration and reporting requirements under City code

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — June 9, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a public hearing on the city's spending plans for the next two fiscal years. The council voted on adopting budgets for capital improvement projects, operating expenses, the Housing Authority, and the Sports and Open Space Authority.
keyTopics: Two-year capital improvement budget (FY 2026/27 & FY 2027/28)
Operating budget changes for FY 2026/27
Housing Authority budget adoption
Sports and Open Space Authority budget adoption

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026 to discuss updates to the city's housing and community development plan for 2025-2030, review new project requests from council members, and consider changes to how the city fills council vacancies. The meeting also covered routine administrative items like approving previous meeting minutes and setting the calendar for upcoming meetings.
keyTopics: Updates to the 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan (housing and federal funding priorities)
New workplan requests from individual councilmembers
Proposed changes to the Council Vacancy Ordinance
Approval of meeting minutes and upcoming meeting schedule

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 7, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a hearing on a proposal to build a six-story apartment building with 72 units at 450 Lytton Avenue on a city parking lot. The project would be 100% affordable housing and is requesting special approvals under state affordable housing laws to allow the dense development on the small parcel.
keyTopics: 72 affordable housing units proposed at 450 Lytton Avenue
Six-story building would replace 52-space city parking lot (Lot T)
Request for density bonus concessions and waivers under state law
Streamlined approval process for affordable housing development
